models.manufacturer_evidence: Is it possible to make an all time fave even better? That's the question...and the challenge. The Puddle Jumper was a genre defining game changer and one of the most popular small wave hybrids of all time. With a plethora of knock-offs and our own growing line up of spin-offs, its evolution and popularity continues. More than a decade in, we still receive custom orders for original Puddle Jumpers, almost daily. The PUDDLE JUMPER STING is inspired by our long time Puddle Jumper collaboration with LIB-Tech, which features a winged squash tail version of the original Puddle Jumper. We wanted to build on this design and take it a step further, incorporating the iconic STING design element. The insertion of the STING came together naturally and with ease. Looking like it always belonged. One of our widest and most stable/playful plan-shapes has now been radicalized with the STING. Placed at 18" (roughly 1/4 up from the tail) it immediately looked like a perfect fit. Enabling a sharper, tighter and more intense turning fulcrum, into this easy riding, playful fun machine. One of the limitations of exceedingly wide hybrids has always been that they are easy to enjoy, but difficult to get truly radical on. The same problem solving that brought the STING to prominence, nearly 50 years ago, is re-applied here, to the OG Puddle Jumper, with amazing results. Bertlemann will love it and Butt
models.tail_notes: squash tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: "A …lost classic, Digitally Re-mastered" The Quiver Killer is one of our all time, best selling and most loved models. Popular around the world. It was one of the original modern hybrid shapes, that worked in all types of conditions, from 2' slop to 6' tubes. An early 5-fin adapted model, it can be adjusted with fins, for waves of all types and sizes. A literal one board quiver, thus the name, Quiver Killer. Its forward outline and fairly wide nose lend plenty of forward momentum, both paddling and once you're on your feet. Drive is not an issue, here. The generous width and continuous curve outline, with a modestly pulled in rounded pin tail give both lift in smaller surf, and speed control, with precise turning and carving, with confidence in better waves. The nose rocker is moderate, pairing well with the width up front and initiates into turns with forward momentum. The tail rocker is lower. Its fast and drivey, featuring a clean concave and enough curve in the rail rocker, to turn well under the rear foot to not feel stiff. This little board proved itself again and again, with its ability to perform in all conditions, under the feet of local and traveling surfers around the world. We had not adjusted the design in years, simply re-sizing for custom orders as retailers slowly stopped stocking them, in lieu of newer models. With the constant updating and releasing of new mod
models.rocker_notes: The nose rocker is moderate, pairing well with the width up front and initiates into turns with forward momentum. The tail rocker is lower.
models.tail_notes: rounded pin tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: Super-fast, forgiving, user friendly retro fun, featuring modern updates and improvements, suitable for almost anyone. RAD RIPPER is the latest in our "Post Modern Retro" collection. A direct spin off from the Retro Ripper, but scaled for small, sub-par, every day surfing. Starting with the Retro Ripper, I lowered the entry rocker a bit (for fast paddle, early entry, easy glide and quickness out the gate), and replaced the speed controlling round tail with a wide, planing squash tail. Lots of lift and surface area for small surf. As a child of '80's surfing, I've always been enamored by the ease of use from that era's performance short boards. The RAD RIPPER retains a noticeably healthy amount of tail rocker, cut through by an aggressive double concave, adding even more lift and squirt under the rear foot. Super-fast, forgiving, user friendly retro fun, featuring modern updates and improvements, suitable for almost anyone. Highlighted features include: • Flat, stable deck, with steep, low apex, tucked soft rails. • Slight vee in nose (to free up the low rocker) into a shallow single concave, into a deep double concave vee, for both speed andcontrol. • Wide, stable squash tail, glides across anything, and holds speed through turns, even in small summer slop. • Forward thickness flow, with wedged "beak" nose, for drive and paddle -ability. ...Lost BlackSheep BlackSheepBuilt board
models.rocker_notes: lowered the entry rocker
models.tail_notes: round tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Rad Zapper by Lost and Cheyne Horan is a small wave speed machine that harkens back to a past era when boards were fast fun and easy to ride, but equally playful in the water. We created this board together, side by side in the shaping room, integrated his "Lazor Zap" design elements and flair into one of our most popular designs, the 1980's influenced Rad Ripper. What we came out with is the Rad Zapper. The Rad Zapper surfboard features: Noticeably flat deck and unique, volume-reducing rail shape. Carried the thickness flow, aggressively into the nose and the tail. Ending with a pronounced beak nose and tapered tail foil. Moved the wide-point back, noticeably pulling in the "no nose" tip and widening the tail, dramatically, making for amazing small wave attributes. A double bump soft swallow "Butt" tail, which helps control the radical, wide tail. A unique deep double concave though the tail, which starts out soft near the stringer then graduates to an almost hook, out near the edge, rocketing water through the tail, while also lending some grab and bite, for control. Lowered entry rocker, for speed and added a soft belly in the nose, to keep it free and loose. A pointed nose, performance board, designed for smaller surf, where you can stand on the tail and turn wherever you like, without running out of speed or floatation. *Note - colours are only available in the specifi
models.rocker_notes: Lowered entry rocker
models.tail_notes: We created this board together, side by side in the shaping room, integrated his "Lazor Zap" design elements and flair into one of our most popular designs, the 1980's influenced Rad Ripper. Carried the thickness flow, aggressively into the nose and the tail.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The vee lessens through the centre and increases again into an accelerated tail rocker, with deep double concave cutting through the tail curve. The continuous vee bottom enables a high-volume board to surf lower in the water and engage the rail. It cleanly cuts through big bumpy chop or stiff offshore conditions and allows for smooth, controlled, rail to rail surfing. It easily remains in control at high speeds, in powerful surf. The outline wide point is pushed forward and pulls tight into the tail. This forward wide point (and thickness flow) make paddling through challenging line ups, and catching large waves, a lot less work. They work together to allow early entry and provide drive and glide in solid waves. The flat deck (with steep, low apex rails) and pulled in tail, provide a stable, yet nimble enough, a platform for precise positioning on large wave faces, and in tubes. Built standard with Strong (yet flexible) 4ply Redwood stringer Thruster/Quad convertible fin set up. *Please note that the price shown reflects the cost for a white board with no spray. The pictured full board spray is available for an extra $11. Full board sprays are popular on gun models as they allow you to keep the board for a long period of time without it aging too quickly. Please call us if you'd like to discuss spray options.
models.rocker_notes: The vee lessens through the centre and increases again into an accelerated tail rocker, with deep double concave cutting through the tail curve. The continuous vee bottom enables a high-volume board to surf lower in the water and engage the rail.
models.tail_notes: The vee lessens through the centre and increases again into an accelerated tail rocker, with deep double concave cutting through the tail curve. The outline wide point is pushed forward and pulls tight into the tail.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Designed as an everyday utility board to semi-step up. A great travel board, that can handle some size, and a wide variety of conditions. The target surfer for this board is the guy who tends to ride wider, thicker, high volume, relaxed rocker boards in small, daily surf...and struggles to find a suitable board for bigger, better, or more challenging days. Very well suited for solid East Coast hurricane and winter swells, crowded head high beach breaks, long winding points and really excels in strong offshore winds, or choppy, windy conditions.
models.rocker_notes: The target surfer for this board is the guy who tends to ride wider, thicker, high volume, relaxed rocker boards in small, daily surf...and struggles to find a suitable board for bigger, better, or more challenging days.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The RETRO TRIPPER is inspired by the desire for a short and wide, with plenty of glide, fish alternative, for surfing punchy and powerful waves with confidence and control. It's wide (beaked) nose, winged pin tail, mellow retro-rocker, flat deck and steep downward rails, combined with Twin+1 fin set-up, excels in good waves, powerful waves, hollow waves, fast waves and big waves, but is still wide and skatey enough for everyday fun. Features Developed in early 2021, first for myself as a board for smaller days on an early season Salina Cruz, March strike mission. My first one worked well, with room to improve, so I made another for April in The Mentawais, while back on the move. At the same time, inspired by my own, we made a few more, for some team riders around home. STAB Electric Acid came with a call, so we made two for the siblings Ho, glassed on fins and all. From these early prototypes, feedback was made...and by July '21, on the road to El Salvador…hoping they passed the grade. With Coco Ho and Luke Davis as the ponies, we scored solid surf and realized The Retro Tripper was money! Based on the RetroRipper chassis, but significantly shorter (with lower entry rocker), thicker, wider and with a forward outline. RETRO TRIPPER is similar to our classic #MonkFish-round pin, ridden by Cory Lopez, in his seminal Skeleton Bay segment, from 5'5" Redux. The Flat deck, forward out
models.rocker_notes: lower entry rocker
models.tail_notes: pin tail

models.source_summary: Manufacturer-described retro fish with modern performance capability.
models.manufacturer_evidence: THE 5' 5" X 19 ¼ RNF IS BACK AND BETTER THAN EVER! The surf industry was in a 20 year performance design rut when in '94 and '95 Matt Biolos experimented with multiple variations of fish for himself, Chris Ward and Cory Lopez that became the building blocks for a performance hybrid revolution. "I'd never before had so many surfers and shapers ask me what are you riding?" The RNF '96 sh designs that teenaged Chris and Cory took to the North Shore and made their indelible mark on the surfing universe were featured in the seminal '97 surf film 5'5" X 19 ¼. The key component of Matt's fish is that they are performance minded inspired by MR's competitive minded twins of the '70s and '80s with relatively pulled in tails designed to rip, not just small surf but in all sizes and shape of waves. Most fish have been predominantly influenced by wide tailed '70s kneeboards that create speed but have always fallen far short of the peak performance of modern short boards. In the name of great surfing and surfboards Matt has applied the past 25 years of board building to create a better surfboard faithfully based on but not exacting to the 96 RNF versions. "With diligent testing being done by Kolohe, Coco, Ian Crane, Crosby Cola and other "guests" including myself in the last few months of 2020, we are very confident that the RNF '96 will perform at and above expectations, as an all-around, r
models.rocker_notes: The RNF 96 features: A traditional fish outline Medium rail profile, increasing versatility Increased rocker with a pulled-in tail to suit performance surfing Foam throughout the centre to help with paddling and entry 2+1 fin setup We suggest riding the Lost RNF 96 at the same litres as your standard high-performance shortboard, which is about 5 inches short
models.tail_notes: The RNF 96 features: A traditional fish outline Medium rail profile, increasing versatility Increased rocker with a pulled-in tail to suit performance surfing Foam throughout the centre to help with paddling and entry 2+1 fin setup We suggest riding the Lost RNF 96 at the same litres as your standard high-performance shortboard, which is about 5 inches short

models.manufacturer_evidence: "The worlds most popular "Fish"...all grown up" By popular demand! The RNF'96_KINGFISH, is here to make all your mid-length (and Mid-life) dreams, come true. We thought about it for years, yet never pulled the trigger. After all these years as the top selling and most popular "Fish" model in the world, the "little disc" is now stretched out (and sleeked up) into our line of Mid-Length Mayhems. Its no secret that many of us who started riding fish in the mid-'90s are now well into our '50s, not surfing every day and distracted by life's trials and tribulations. We've grown up…and now, our favorite board has grown up, too. Containing all the proven design details and DNA as the RNF_96, from which is "spawned", the RNF'96_KINGFISH features: Low entry rocker, for paddling through line-ups, ease in catching waves and plenty of glide, to take you where you wanna go, once your on one. Flattish deck, with full, down/soft rails, for floatation and stability. The flat deck allows us to stay relatively thin, with the KINGFISH, keeping the deck-line curvy. Even the larger sizes are relatively nimble, flexible and forgiving, underfoot. Balanced outline, with modest width in the nose tail, which both measure the same width, on all sizes, retaining plenty of central curve in the outline, for turning. The tail is still similar width to a shortboard and combined with the shallow swallow, it doe
models.rocker_notes: Low entry rocker
models.tail_notes: Containing all the proven design details and DNA as the RNF_96, from which is "spawned", the RNF'96_KINGFISH features: Low entry rocker, for paddling through line-ups, ease in catching waves and plenty of glide, to take you where you wanna go, once your on one. The flat deck allows us to stay relatively thin, with the KINGFISH, keeping the deck-line curvy.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Introducing Lost x LongToe lamination PU constructed surfboards—unique, one-of-a-kind, crafted creations. Boasting LongToe's unique artistry in a smooth and bright finish on the bottom and rails with handcrafted pin line sanded decks. These boards are more than just watercraft—they're captivating pieces of rideable art that turn heads both in and out of the water. With each board being unique and one-of-a-kind, owning a Lost x LongToe lamination surfboard means owning a unique piece of art that also rips in the surf. The Lost/Mayhem RNF 96 is back and better than ever. The board that launched Matt Biolos' career has long captivated surfers around the world, particularly after the surfing done on the model by Corey Lopez, Andy Irons, Chris Ward and the rest of the Lost crew in the groundbreaking film 5'5" x 19 1/4". It was fast, loose and great fun to ride in smaller surf. Matt has refined the original shape to enhance its performance capabilities, producing a retro fish that really goes. This board has been the talk of the town after first appearing in Hawaii in the 20/21 winter. Many pro surfers (including those who don't ride for Lost) have been clamouring to get on the RNF 96 to try it out and now it's your turn! If you're looking for a small wave weapon that will keep a smile on your face even when the waves are less than stellar, then look no further.
models.rocker_notes: Boasting LongToe's unique artistry in a smooth and bright finish on the bottom and rails with handcrafted pin line sanded decks.
models.tail_notes: Boasting LongToe's unique artistry in a smooth and bright finish on the bottom and rails with handcrafted pin line sanded decks.

models.manufacturer_evidence: For the RNF-REDUX, we set about blending the best of both boards. Keeping the pulled in performance and easy-going drive of the Classic, with glide and versatility of the RNF-Quint, we created a high-performance hybrid that paddles well and glides across flat-faced, sloppy, small junk surf, as well as perform radical, on rail, precise high-speed surfing in real waves. Features The rocker, bottom contours and forward outline are based on the Classic. The tail outline is a hybrid of both. An MR influenced, fluted wing, transitions into a narrower swallow. The deep wing and pulled in tail reduce surface area and supply that precise tack sharp feeling when turning in the pocket. The added area in front of the wing, makes a more parallel outline between the feet, providing planing, drive and glide. Like all versions of the RNF, the REDUX features a very low overall centerline rocker, with modest single concave under the front foot, to keep the board planing at low speeds. The concave gradually reduces to flat up in the nose, allowing forgiving entry into waves and in and out of turns. The vee in the tail enables the board to retain its very low stringer-line rocker, while still being easy to turn, and gives the board so much control. The radical change from concave in the centre, to vee in the tail creates an aggressive rail rocker that engages when the board is rolled onto a rail.
models.rocker_notes: Keeping the pulled in performance and easy-going drive of the Classic, with glide and versatility of the RNF-Quint, we created a high-performance hybrid that paddles well and glides across flat-faced, sloppy, small junk surf, as well as perform radical, on rail, precise high-speed surfing in real waves. Features The rocker, bottom contours and forward outlin
models.tail_notes: Keeping the pulled in performance and easy-going drive of the Classic, with glide and versatility of the RNF-Quint, we created a high-performance hybrid that paddles well and glides across flat-faced, sloppy, small junk surf, as well as perform radical, on rail, precise high-speed surfing in real waves. The tail outline is a hybrid of both.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The RNF Retro is our latest spin on the always evolving Round Nose Fish. It features noticeable elements from the purist (mostly San Diego influenced) evolution of wide, split tailed Fish, and smashes them together with much of what we have learned in nearly 25 years of shaping and riding the ...Lost RNF. First off, the tail is wide for that effortless glide and retro vibe. Coming in at over 1 inches from tip to tip, and 4 inches deep, it's the deepest and widest split tail we've released. The board prominently features a snowboard-esque, double side cut outline. Strongly influenced by years of snowboarding, and then seeing the boards of underground SD shapers like Mirandon and Caster, we first developed and released our double side cut on the Pelagic model, in the late 2's. Like with a snowboard, the sidecut 'grips' the riding surface and allows for more radical, tighter radius direction changes on wider, otherwise difficult to turn sharply, outlines and flat rockers. The sidecut hooks into the wave face and turns surprisingly sharp, without the usual drift or skip or slip, associated with common wide tail fish shapes. The flat deck allows the board to be ridden thinner, which enables more flex. Thin flat decks maintain more scoop in the deck rocker, which when surfed with the rail buried perpendicular into the surface, helps low rocker boards turn in tighter arcs, rather than
models.rocker_notes: Like with a snowboard, the sidecut 'grips' the riding surface and allows for more radical, tighter radius direction changes on wider, otherwise difficult to turn sharply, outlines and flat rockers. Thin flat decks maintain more scoop in the deck rocker, which when surfed with the rail buried perpendicular into the surface, helps low rocker boards turn in tig
models.tail_notes: The RNF Retro is our latest spin on the always evolving Round Nose Fish. It features noticeable elements from the purist (mostly San Diego influenced) evolution of wide, split tailed Fish, and smashes them together with much of what we have learned in nearly 25 years of shaping and riding the ...Lost RNF.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The RNF TWINZER '96er The latest incarnation of our definitive fish design, the RNF-'96 enhanced with our TWINZER+ fin cluster. Based off the RNF-'96, a 2021 re-vamped version of our original 5'5" Round Nose Fish (developed 30 years ago), it's been our best-selling board of the past five years and arguably the best-selling "fish" model of all time. The TWINZER '96er combines all the proven performance design details of the RNF-'96 with the added grip, control, and drive of the TWINZER+ fin cluster. All are descendants of the original hand-shaped boards, first ridden by teenaged Chris Ward and Cory Lopez 30 years ago and documented in the seminal surf flick 5'5" x 19 1/4—together making an indelible mark on the surfing universe. The Roots of the TWINZER+ The impetus for the TWINZER+ has been building for over 25 years. It goes back to 1999 and the Rusty Surfboards "C5 Challenge"—a surfing/shaper competition at our home break, Lower Trestles. Rusty had been creating and selling his own take on the Twinzer, using small canard fins strategically positioned on tri-fin/thruster boards. Thus the namesake "C-5," because of the five-fin setup. The C5 Challenge invited shapers and surfers to enter as teams, and "challenged" us to build boards using Rusty's formula: five fins, with the forward Twinzer-esque canards being the unique element. We entered with Dino Andino and Shea Lopez on bo
models.tail_notes: The TWINZER '96er combines all the proven performance design details of the RNF-'96 with the added grip, control, and drive of the TWINZER+ fin cluster.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Rocket redux Now in Libtech tech. Before mass media marketeers played Hypto-Crypto with your mind, making you believe in the Sci-Fi that Ghosts and Monstas were more than just Average Joes, Diving through Dumpsters along the Seaside...there was The ROCKET!...and we ain't talking 9 or Wide. Before Instagram and the endless charade of social media surfboard model marketing melted our minds, real word of mouth made one little board iconic throughout the surfing world....The ROCKET. Birthed from a broken blank and the brains of Beschen (Shane) and Budroe, the original Rocket was to simply bea board for baby Beschen (Noah) to learn on.Once ridden by Shane, everything changed, and he felt compelled to bring us his fabulous find to be refined. Happy accidents have been a staple of surfboard progression, since the beginning.As we started fine tuning and building them for the boys, we knew were had something special.Fast forward a year, and the 29 video release of 5'5"Redux. The ROCKET was a star of the show, ridden by a parade of guest rippers cutting loose on the little weapon. From Bros to Pros, The ROCKET was soon a global hit, being enjoyed by surfers of all shapes, sizes and skill sets. Now, more than a decade after its initial release, the ROCKET returns. A re-incarnated Redux for the new generation to enjoy another run of fun... The ROCKET REDUX We've strived to retain all the o
models.rocker_notes: low and fast entry rocker
models.tail_notes: The ROCKET REDUX We've strived to retain all the original ROCKET design details and magic: The extremely low and fast entry rocker, counterbalanced by a hyper-extended tail curve, for instantaneousdown the line drive and playfully precise turning.The

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Round Up by Lost Surfboards is a great option for when the waves are pumping but the Rock Up is a little too gunny. Keeping the same aggressive rockers and outline, this board has been tweaked to include slightly fuller rails that are more forgiving. The Lost Round UP is a great fun step up option when the waves are pumping but can also perform when they're just above average. Great for controlling speed in head high and larger surf and hollow more powerful waves.
models.rocker_notes: Keeping the same aggressive rockers and outline, this board has been tweaked to include slightly fuller rails that are more forgiving.
models.tail_notes: The Round Up by Lost Surfboards is a great option for when the waves are pumping but the Rock Up is a little too gunny. Keeping the same aggressive rockers and outline, this board has been tweaked to include slightly fuller rails that are more forgiving.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The latest in our quest for the ultimate "Domesticated" design. The RV is exactly as its name implies… A "Recreational Vehicle". Designed for extracting maximum fun with minimum effort and ease of exploitation of your precious free time… If you want to cruise the coast in style and comfort, surf in a resplendent state of wave catching excess and bliss, then the R.V. is for you. The design stems from a blend of the Bottom Feeder/Couch Potato/LayZtoy genre. Noticeable updates include the double wing flyers added to the tail, to reduce surface area and increase bite and precision in turns. The reduced surface area adds confidence of not sliding out in hard snaps, and carves. The nose outline has also been tweaked to reduce some are in the forward 12-18″ of the board, but without reducing drive down the line or paddle ability. The nose feels like it "comes around" quicker in turns and looks …and feels, like more of a performance design. Get on an R.V. and enjoy your recreational time like you never knew you could!
models.tail_notes: Noticeable updates include the double wing flyers added to the tail, to reduce surface area and increase bite and precision in turns.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Sabotaj is the result of ongoing design development between Taj Burrow and Matt Biolos. Meant to perform in the widest range of conditions by minimalistic means, and essentially provide Taj a one board weapon for his last minute, whirlwind travels ...where theres no time, space or desire to pack a proper quiver. The Sabotaj is equally at home in 2' shore break as 8' Indo, and everything in between. Taj rides it 1" shorter, but a touch wider, than his typical proformance shortboard, but depending on your preferred use, it can be ridden even a bit shorter (or much longer) for various, and equally effective degrees of diversity and fun. We began with the Lost Rock-Up chassis, and made refinements and tweaks (based off TBs input and feedback over several boards) until it became its own entity altogether. From the Rock-Up, to Round Up, to the Mini Driver, and more than 1 years of developing shorter, wide nose, low entry "quiver killing" travel boards, we feel like this is the best one yet. The Sabotaj features a modest, overall rocker that is "shifted back", so that the entry is low, fast and drivey. The tail rocker has the curve and release of a high performance step up for tight radius whip and control. The rail line creates a shallow concave under and between feet to add lift and freedom, but zeros out in the nose and tail, to keep things catch free, forgiving, settled and pr
models.rocker_notes: The Sabotaj features a modest, overall rocker that is "shifted back", so that the entry is low, fast and drivey. The tail rocker has the curve and release of a high performance step up for tight radius whip and control.
models.tail_notes: From the Rock-Up, to Round Up, to the Mini Driver, and more than 1 years of developing shorter, wide nose, low entry "quiver killing" travel boards, we feel like this is the best one yet. The tail rocker has the curve and release of a high performance step up for tight radius whip and control.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Smooth Operator is a trim and turn machine. High volume, low rails and tons of fun. We have souped them up with a 5 fin set up, offering a myriad of options and performance characteristics. More recently, we developed the Crowd Killer, more of a pump and rip stick, with its hyper extended nose rocker and equally aggressive tail rocker. While high volume and performance-minded, the Crowd Killer does not possess the effortless glide and horizontal trim of a classic mid-length. The Smooth Operator does. Built around an exaggeratedly low entry rocker, forward wide point and thickness, and a nose and tail that measure the same width at both 12 and 24, the Smooth Operator paddles and glides like nothing ever adorned with a ...Lost or Mayhem logo. That's not to say it doesn't turn though! After riding, dissecting and assessing some contemporary and classic mid lengths currently available, I concluded they all could use a bit more tail rocker! The Smooth Operator has just that. A generous (dare say extreme) tail rocker, bends upward in the rear third of the board.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The SPEED DEMON was an original …Lost Surfboards model—a model synonymous with full-on, pedal-to-the-metal, furiously fast, small-wave surfing. Originally developed with feedback from San Clemente Mafia maestro, Dino Andino, in the mid-1990s, it became one of our early, well-known models. Tuned at the long, sloping, flattish-faced waves of Trestles, with design details drawn from decades of down-the-line speed runs and big, head-snapping hacks. With its speed-centric low rocker, plenty of width and thickness, deep concave, and some vee running through the rear of Dino's signature extra-wide tail block, the SPEED DEMON was one of the first models that we successfully sold through surf shops around the country and across the world. Cut to Spring 2025 Dino's son, longtime design muse Kolohe, was focused on simply surfing local summer slop and occasional quality days around town. On small days, bouncing between Puddle Jumpers, Sub-Drivers, and Rad Rippers—all to great effect—with an emphasis on sustained speed for freedom to do whatever you want on lackluster waves. At one point we handed him a Rad Zapper, our collab with Cheyne Horan. At Trestles, he laid down some truly inspiring, classic, calm, cool power surfing with vicious flair and finesse, that rapidly went viral. As summer progressed, Kolohe came with a challenge: "I want something that sits between the Rippers, Rad Ripper

models.manufacturer_evidence: Simply put, the Step Driver is a high-performance step-up, directly evolved from the Driver 2.0. Designed for the highest level of performance surfing, where critical manoeuvres and tube riding meet. Meant to be ridden 1-2" (or more, if customised) longer, similar width and a bit thicker, than a Driver 2.0 or similar HP shortboard, the Step Driver rides like a seamless transition from your everyday board, without sacrificing performance for control. Tested and proven in powerful surf around the world, by WCT surfers such as Olympians, Kolohe Andino, Carissa Moore and Caroline Marks, as well as Griffin Colapinto, Yago Dora and free surfers like Mason Ho and Ian Crane and up-n-comers like Crosby Colapinto and Eli Hanneman. It's a one-stop-shop, for the needs of our entire team. The Step Driver allows surfers to ride larger, more powerful and more challenging waves as if they were head high and playful, on a board that is usually only 1-2" longer than their everyday board. Anyone with a Driver 2.0 in their quiver (or similar high-performance shortboard), who is looking for that perfect board to transition to when the waves get special (or if you're lucky to live somewhere like Hawaii or Indo, where these waves are the norm) will be pleased to have the Step Driver in their arsenal. Using the Driver 2.0 as a starting point, design elements and adaptations include: Increased tail roc
models.rocker_notes: Using the Driver 2.0 as a starting point, design elements and adaptations include: Increased tail rocker, blended smooth, to fit the curve of and control speed in larger, powerful and/or hollow waves. A significantly shallower single concave throughout, which reduces lift and smooths out the rail lin

models.manufacturer_evidence: Good surfers say it pushes back, even in small waves. Lost's Sub-Driver not only benefits Pros, it really is perfect for most Bros. Short and stubby with plenty of built-in drive, it's easy to catch waves and generate speed on.

Our best small wave, performance shortboard just got better! Since its inception, each version of the SUB DRIVER has remained our most successful, small wave competition surfboard. From Kolohe Andino's 2011 run of 5 WQS event wins (WCT qualification) to Mick Fanning taking notice and famously grabbing one of Kolohe's discarded SUB DRIVERS, blazing through heats at The Lowers Pro and subsequently claiming the board had "Completely changed his perception of what a competition board can be". The SUB DRIVER has remained a mainstay of our competitive team rider's quivers. Year after year, competitors around the world request the SUB DRIVER as a special weapon for the varied and unpredictable WQS surf, yet just about anyone with even modest skills can ride this board as a daily driver. The SUB DRIVER 2.0 is the latest iteration in the continuing evolution of our most user friendly, pro-formance shortboard. Developed in the soft peeling points of San Clemente, it loves small to average surf and really does well in connecting the dots in mushy points, or weak, broken up beach breaks. A surfboard developed for competitors in sub-par surf, that actually transitions perfectly to everyday surfers. The SUB DRIVER 2.0 was derived to benefit the Pros, yet it really is perfect for most "Bros". Reflecting the small wave demands from Kolohe Andino, Griffin Colapinto and our up and coming team of
models.tail_notes: Reflecting the small wave demands from Kolohe Andino, Griffin Colapinto and our up and coming team of Jr Pros and WQS Warriors, constant evolution and noticeable updates have brought us to the point where we are now proud to launch the SUB DRIVER 2.0.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Lost Sub-Driver 3.0 was born from Matt Biolos' consistent work with the new generation of surfers on the World Tour. He mas made some subtle refinements in the foil, bottom curves, outline rails and rocker have allowed the Sub-Driver 3.0 to continue to be one of the best small wave surfboards. The Sub-Driver 3.0 is a performance step-down surfboard, for high-speed, small-wave power surfing. The Lost Sub-Driver 3.0 surfboard features: Low tail rocker, curvy rail line creating concave and vee out the back, is a winning small wave combination. Excels in average surf for the everyday surfer who wants to ride a performance shortboard. A performance outline, with its wide point set back, creating a hip between the feet, that swoops into a moderately wide swallow. The bottom features a deep single concave, through the center, maxing out between the feet, then hyper-extends (with plenty of double concave) through the tail. There, the rail line accelerates to the Sub Driver 3.0's distinctive Vee, behind the rear fin. The swallow tail reduces surface area, provides drive and lends some grip and pivot, while still retaining width, for speed. The rail rocker mirrors the outline hip, combining to provide a balanced fulcrum to initiate effortless turns, anywhere on the wave. This is really where the board has evolved to the point of warranting a new model update. The low, fast tail rocke
models.rocker_notes: moderate entry rocker
models.tail_notes: swallow tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Sub Driver Swallow loves small to moderate surf. It is stable, with plenty of built in drive, and easy to catch waves and generate speed on. The wide nose and tail make for a stable platform in small waves and at low speeds. Combined with low overall rocker and full rails, the board can be ridden pretty short. The Sub Driver Swallow not only benefits the Pros, it really is perfect for most of the Bros too. Over the course of 218, we found repeated team success with a new Hip Swallow tail, and are now offering them for 219. Somewhat radical in its design, its most prominent features being the slightly wider hip and tail block, and noticeable spiral vee behind the rear fin. The full nose provides stability for landing airs. The wide hip, and tail block creates a sort of bite to the wide tail. It works well with hyper extended rail rocker and spiral vee, to allow the Sub Driver Swallow to be surfed more tightly in the pocket than the traditional squash tail. The wide nose and tail make for a stable platform in small waves and at low speeds. Combined with low overall rocker and full rails, the board can be ridden pretty short. The Sub Driver Swallow not only benefits the Pros, it really is perfect for most of the Bros too. The Sub Driver Swallow loves small to moderate surf. It is stable, with plenty of built in drive, and easy to catch waves and generate speed on.

models.manufacturer_evidence: A ...lost classic. The Sub Scorcher is a relaxed rocker, conservatively outlined, higher volumed, easy riding spin on the modern performance shortboard. A central concave to spiral-vee in the tail, small wave specific design. Under the feet of young teen phenom, Kolohe Andino, the OG Sub Scorcher was a break out hit, during its initial run. It has remained a custom order mainstay, ever since. With its mellow rocker, wide, stable outline, shallow single to double, to vee in the tail, the Sub Scorcher is as easy paddling, easy planing and easy riding as a modern, pointed nose shortboard gets. We started with our OG Sub Scorcher swallow and introduced the STING. We added a bit of width through the center, some extra rail rocker and injected the radical, area reducing STING at about 18" (1/4 of the way up) from the tail. The STING is followed by a gradual tail curve, into a 6" wide, shallow swallow. The extra width in center assures easy entry and quick planing out of the gate. The STING makes a sharp reduction in width and creates an acute angle for jamming quick, radical, controlled turns into tight sections, or out on the waves open face. Generously dimensioned, with plenty of volume, the SUB SCORCHER-STING, is an every mans, playful, performance shortboard, with the STING design element allowing easy initiation of radical turns and maneuvers.
models.rocker_notes: The Sub Scorcher is a relaxed rocker, conservatively outlined, higher volumed, easy riding spin on the modern performance shortboard. With its mellow rocker, wide, stable outline, shallow single to double, to vee in the tail, the Sub Scorcher is as easy paddling, easy planing and easy riding as a modern, pointed nose shortboard gets.
models.tail_notes: The Sub Scorcher is a relaxed rocker, conservatively outlined, higher volumed, easy riding spin on the modern performance shortboard. A central concave to spiral-vee in the tail, small wave specific design.

models.manufacturer_evidence: SWORD-FISH : A fast, aggressive and powerful predatory 'Fish', with a longer, pointed nose, side cut 'Pelagic' outline, wide fish tail and ridiculously fast rocker. The SWORD-FISH is precisely as the name implies: A furiously fast Pelagic fish, aggressive, with a wide powerful tail, and a long-pointed nose. Designed at the request of (and direction from) Kolohe Andino, as an extension of his favourite RNF-Retro. Kolohe surfed his little neon RNF-Retro better than I'd ever seen anyone surf any fish in small Trestles waves. Inspired, yet not satisfied, he came to me wanting to create something even better. I love that little Retro-fish. It works way better than I ever would have thought, but I can't get my head completely around that short little nose. If that thing had a longer nose on it, I could probably ride it in contests. I really want that fish with a longer, more pulled nose. Make it about 5'9", so it feels more like a real shortboard up front. Maybe a bit narrower overall. I want a longer rail, with more drive off my front foot. More power, a bit stiffer, more aggressive and even faster. Ridiculously fast. You can leave everything else kinda the same."- KA Gauntlet laid!. So we went to work and to do just that. Starting with Kolohe's magic little 5'6" RNF-Retro we added 3" of length, by extending the nose, pulling it in and added some gradual nose rocker. We also narrowe
models.rocker_notes: SWORD-FISH : A fast, aggressive and powerful predatory 'Fish', with a longer, pointed nose, side cut 'Pelagic' outline, wide fish tail and ridiculously fast rocker. I want a longer rail, with more drive off my front foot.
models.tail_notes: SWORD-FISH : A fast, aggressive and powerful predatory 'Fish', with a longer, pointed nose, side cut 'Pelagic' outline, wide fish tail and ridiculously fast rocker. The SWORD-FISH is precisely as the name implies: A furiously fast Pelagic fish, aggressive, with a wide powerful tail, and a long-pointed nose.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Alex Lopez mastered the art of speed, style and flow in the powder fields of Mt Bachelor. He studied the art of shaping under the wing of his father and some of the best board builders of our time. Alex has dedicated the last decade of his life to the shaping room, refining his skills and perfecting his surf craft one board at a time with his test pilot partner Leah Dawson and a crew of talented free-surfers. The Terrapin is Alex's personal favorite ride. Designed to be a versatile wave catching one-board quiver that excels in all conditions at your home breaks but you can confidently push into larger quality surf at quality wave destinations around the world. This agile "modern classic" wide point forward single fin is built for smooth surfing and glide. Alex's magically sculpted vision in our dang difficult to ding construction is sure to relax your lifestyle and take your surfing to new dimensions. "Built for glide, wide point forward, longer rail line for smooth surfing. My personal favorite board." - A. Lopez
models.rocker_notes: "Built for glide, wide point forward, longer rail line for smooth surfing.
models.tail_notes: He studied the art of shaping under the wing of his father and some of the best board builders of our time. Alex has dedicated the last decade of his life to the shaping room, refining his skills and perfecting his surf craft one board at a time with his test pilot partner Leah Dawson and a crew of talented free-surfers.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Lost Puddle Jumper is one of Lost Surfboards most loved models of all time. It was one of the original, modern "groveler" specific shapes, that caught fire around the world. The Lost Puddle Jumper surfboard features: Its significant width, low rocker, forward outline, with wide nose and tail, make paddling, catching waves and skating along on small waves a breeze. Fast and drivey, with plenty of lift, the magic comes from the hyper-curved rail line rocker, which runs parallel to the stringer, near the nose, then exaggerates into a deep single concave, that swoops back down to a significant vee, under the rear foot. This high arcing rail line, with a double concave vee in the tail, creates a lively ride and turns the relatively flat board into a turning machine, especially once put on rail. There's enough curve in the accelerating rail rocker, to loosen up and transform The Original Puddle Jumper, from a typical flat little plank into a mini-performance machine, allowing turns in 1'-2' waves that you wouldn't believe you could do. The original Puddle Jumper has proven itself again and again in smaller waves, with surfers of multiple skill sets. This subtle update is no different. The branding has been brought up to pace with modern taste with subdued, stylish colours built into smaller-sized logos.
models.rocker_notes: The Lost Puddle Jumper surfboard features: Its significant width, low rocker, forward outline, with wide nose and tail, make paddling, catching waves and skating along on small waves a breeze. models.tail_notes: The Lost Puddle Jumper surfboard features: Its significant width, low rocker, forward outline, with wide nose and tail, make paddling, catching waves and skating along on small waves a breeze. This high arcing rail line, with a double concave vee in the tail, creates a lively ride and turns the relatively flat board into a turning machine, especially once pu

models.manufacturer_evidence: "2024's runaway small wave shortboard hit… The RIPPER, is now available with a round tail." The round tail gives The RIPPER more range in a wider variety of waves than the original squash tail excelled in. Reducing surface area in the last few inches of the tail lends more control, at higher speeds and allows more precise turns in tighter, powerful pockets. With only a minimal loss of lift and effectiveness in the smallest surf, the RIPPER Rnd makes up for it, with increased effectiveness once the surf gets a bit more size or punch. Whether it's long drawn out carves on overhead walls or quick snaps and gouges in curvy, dumpy waves, the round tail elevates The RIPPER beyond being a small wave, grovel specialized surfboard. The rocker, rails and bottom remain relatively unchanged, but the nose is just slightly narrower, and the tail foiled out a bit, as well. We set up the stock dims to be ridden 1", or so longer than the original, squash tail, version, for an effective a One-Two punch, mini quiver, in most the surf you will see in a typical town.
models.rocker_notes: The rocker, rails and bottom remain relatively unchanged, but the nose is just slightly narrower, and the tail foiled out a bit, as well.
models.tail_notes: round tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: THE RIPPER! A rip-roaring, radically fun small-wave slasher, carved out from the ridiculously popular Rad Ripper. This board is built to tear apart everyday, average surf and leave neon '80s nostalgia washed up on the sand. The RIPPER was inspired by Kolohe Andino, whose love for (and performances on) the Rad Ripper are undeniable. Hooked on the Rad Ripper, Kolohe wanted a version with a more modern feel—something that ditched the retro '80s vibe in favour of a contemporary, high-performance design. The result? An all-new small-wave shred sled. To achieve this, we scrapped the beak nose and refined the entire deck line, swapping out the flat deck and steep rails for a more contemporary, moderately rolled rail and deck. The tail is scooped out with a thinner, boxy block. We also removed the deep fluted wing, replacing it with a subtle bump/hip while maintaining plenty of small-wave surface area. The nose has been slightly pulled in with a touch more rocker, making it fit better into tight transitions. Like its predecessor, the Rad Ripper, The RIPPER retains an overall low rocker with a single concave through the centre, transitioning into a deep double concave/vee through the fins and out the tail. The built-in speed, paddle power, and carving control that made the Rad Ripper a favourite are all carried through in this update. The RIPPER thrives when surfed aggressively off the
models.rocker_notes: To achieve this, we scrapped the beak nose and refined the entire deck line, swapping out the flat deck and steep rails for a more contemporary, moderately rolled rail and deck. The nose has been slightly pulled in with a touch more rocker, making it fit better into tight transitions.
models.tail_notes: To achieve this, we scrapped the beak nose and refined the entire deck line, swapping out the flat deck and steep rails for a more contemporary, moderately rolled rail and deck. The tail is scooped out with a thinner, boxy block.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Tube Pig has been designed with Taj Burrow for surfing in his local big Western Australia waves. This model combines a nose that has more width than its rounded pin tail to increase paddle power while also maximising front foot drive. With the pulled in tail, this board also offers you plenty of control even with a lower than normal, but still typically high performance rocker. The Taj Burrow Tube Pig by Mayhem is going to be your next go to barrel board for big barreling pumping waves. For best results. try the Tube Pig around the same size if not a little bigger than your standard shortboard.
models.rocker_notes: With the pulled in tail, this board also offers you plenty of control even with a lower than normal, but still typically high performance rocker.
models.tail_notes: rounded pin tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: If you thought the Uber Driver was a super fun, forgiving performance board then just wait until you try the Uber Driver LightSpeed. Surf faster, tighter and stronger. The Uber Driver in LightSpeed features; the outline and rocker/concaves from the back half (engine room/control centre) of the DRIVERS currently ridden in smaller waves on tour by Kolohe Andino and Griffin Colapinto. It is then blended to a lower, wider, faster, more forgiving and playful nose. The tail features a low centreline rocker with a curvy rail line rocker under the rear foot, that transitions from a generous single/double concave through the front fins, to a noticeable vee behind the rear fin and out the tail. The nose rocker and forward outline was imported from our Rocket series outlines, and allows this board to be ridden shorter, with more speed out the gate and more drive and stability for everyday surfers. The outline and rocker are blended in a way that enables the board to still be surfed like a modern Pro-formance shortboard. It just feels like you're cheating a little. The Uber-Driver is the Ripper's Pro-formance hybrid. Uber-ripping fun for everyone. LightSpeed EPS Tech Light Speed is our proprietary surfboard construction built with lightweight EPS foam and high strength epoxy resin. LightSpeed uses a combination of Rapid-Reflex Carbon Fiber as well vibration dampening Innegra-Fibers, woven
models.rocker_notes: Shaper Matt 'Mayhem' Biolos explains, "The Uber Driver features the outline and rocker/concaves from the back half of the Driver.It is then blended to a lower, wider, faster, more forgiving and playful nose. The nose rocker and forward outline was imported from our Rocket series outlines, and allows this board to be ridden shorter, with more speed out the ga
models.tail_notes: Initially based off the wildly popular Driver Proformance , the Uber Driver has then been squashed down and fattened out a little to bridge the gap between ultra high performance and small wave hybrids like the Puddle Jumper HP and the V3 Rocket .

models.manufacturer_evidence: For the guy who knows how to surf, prefers a modern Pro-Formance rocker, but needs a bit of help in the crowded line ups and sloppy surf. Flatter, faster (slightly fatter) and funner. You won't have a bummer...Winter, Spring, Fall or Summer By popular demand! When developing the Uber Driver, we overlooked the Domesticated and distinguished Gentlemen..aka bigger boys...My bad! Not anymore. The Uber Driver XL is just as the name says: A scaled up version of the recently re-vamped Uber Driver. The Uber Driver XL is designed for bigger or domesticated surfers, as well as intermediate surfers looking for a stepping stone to a more performance type of surfboard. The rocker, outline and bottom curves are performance minded, and directly off the standard Uber Driver (which in turn came from the Sub-Driver). We then enlarged the dimensions and carried the thickness fow forward. The deck has been adjusted to a fatter, more stable platform, with a low apex tapered rail, that hides the volume, and still permeates the water in turns. For the guy who knows how to surf, prefers a modern Pro-Formance rocker, but needs a bit of help in the crowded line ups and sloppy surf. Flatter, faster (slightly fatter) and funner. You won't have a bummer... Winter, Spring, Fall or Summer.
models.rocker_notes: For the guy who knows how to surf, prefers a modern Pro-Formance rocker, but needs a bit of help in the crowded line ups and sloppy surf. The rocker, outline and bottom curves are performance minded, and directly off the standard Uber Driver (which in turn came from the Sub-Driver).

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Lost V3 Rocket has been designed to perform in a range of conditions and wave types providing the user with a go-to one board quiver. The board has a double v-wing tail, this reduced width behind the back foot enables precise control when surfing tight in the pocket. Mayhem designed the V3 Rocket to function in a wider range of wave sizes. Mayhem recommends this board as a 5-fin as it goes great as both a quad and a thruster helping you keep control in all conditions. All-round performance surfing the V3 Rocket by Mayhem is great for the one board surfer or those travelling and surfing a range of conditions. The V3 Rocket is a high volume shortboard, designed as a refined hybrid it will become your trusty go-to option. The V3 Rocket high-performance characteristics make it the easy surfing fish style model for surfers who don't ride fish.
models.tail_notes: The board has a double v-wing tail, this reduced width behind the back foot enables precise control when surfing tight in the pocket. Mayhem recommends this board as a 5-fin as it goes great as both a quad and a thruster helping you keep control in all conditions.

As the name suggests, it has the same gaseous qualities as early evening Chanel perfume, late-night sea fog and loud sneezes at dusk.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Pegaso is Chris Christenson's more performance-based twin fin, with a more vertical approach than the Chris Fish while keeping twin-fin glide and drive. It can be surfed aggressively in the pocket, still carries speed, and handles surf from 1ft to 5ft, from local beach breaks to Indo. Pukas positions it as a twin you can size 3-4 inches longer than a standard fish or 3-4 inches shorter than a daily performance shortboard.
models.tail_notes: Pegaso is Chris Christenson's more performance-based twin fin, with a more vertical approach than the Chris Fish while keeping twin-fin glide and drive.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The All-Round Longboard is built to work in all wave conditions and stay easy to surf. It carries slightly more tail rocker for sharper, more vertical manoeuvres, blending into a smooth medium nose rocker. The bottom contour uses three transitions: a slightly accentuated full concave in the nose, a smooth middle section and a V-bottom with a more defined double concave through the tail. That layout is intended to keep the board moving efficiently while still supporting movement around the board.
models.rocker_notes: It carries slightly more tail rocker for sharper, more vertical manoeuvres, blending into a smooth medium nose rocker.
models.tail_notes: It carries slightly more tail rocker for sharper, more vertical manoeuvres, blending into a smooth medium nose rocker. The bottom contour uses three transitions: a slightly accentuated full concave in the nose, a smooth middle section and a V-bottom with a more defined double concave through the tail.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Beachy Mood is a daily surf companion built for easy, versatile performance surfing, especially in beach breaks. It uses a subtly curved outline that is slightly wider than high-performance pro dimensions, plus a lazy rocker that increases water contact and natural speed. The bottom runs a medium-depth single concave into double concave between the fins with a soft tail exit, helping rail-to-rail transitions stay smooth while holding speed through flatter sections. It suits small to medium beach-break waves and is recommended one inch shorter than your usual daily board.
models.rocker_notes: It uses a subtly curved outline that is slightly wider than high-performance pro dimensions, plus a lazy rocker that increases water contact and natural speed. The bottom runs a medium-depth single concave into double concave between the fins with a soft tail exit, helping rail-to-rail transitions stay smooth while holding speed through flatter sections.
models.tail_notes: The bottom runs a medium-depth single concave into double concave between the fins with a soft tail exit, helping rail-to-rail transitions stay smooth while holding speed through flatter sections.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Easy Mood is the easiest member of the Mood family and is designed as a step-down to ride around two inches shorter than an everyday performance board. Its curved outline, generous nose area and wide tail help it work in softer conditions without giving away performance. A super deep full concave keeps speed constant, while a lazy rocker balances ease and response. Well-distributed chest thickness improves paddling, wave catching and speed generation, making it a user-friendly performance board for all surfer types once the volume is matched correctly.
models.rocker_notes: A super deep full concave keeps speed constant, while a lazy rocker balances ease and response.
models.tail_notes: Its curved outline, generous nose area and wide tail help it work in softer conditions without giving away performance.

models.manufacturer_evidence: An old-school fish adapted to modern surfing. With an outline reminiscent of a classic old-school fish but slightly narrower throughout, this board offers enhanced agility for executing manoeuvres in the pocket. A more pronounced rocker than a standard fish makes vertical manoeuvres easier. It runs a smooth full concave through the midsection into a V-bottom through the tail with a subtle double concave between the fins. Ultra-low rails help the board slice cleanly through the wave face. Best paired with keel fins, it is ideal for surfers of all skill levels and a wide range of conditions.
models.rocker_notes: A more pronounced rocker than a standard fish makes vertical manoeuvres easier. Ultra-low rails help the board slice cleanly through the wave face.
models.tail_notes: It runs a smooth full concave through the midsection into a V-bottom through the tail with a subtle double concave between the fins.
models.change_reason: current primaryFamily=Performance Fish; \bperformance fish\b; fish in model name

models.manufacturer_evidence: Flying Diamond takes Magnetic DNA and pushes it into a more performance-oriented twin. The outline has more curve and the rocker is medium, while the projected diamond tail keeps the board stable and flowing between turns. The board is designed for strong paddling, modern twin-fin feel and more projection through turns, making it a good fit for surfers who want an easy-riding board with lively performance across travel waves and beach breaks. Pukas recommends a few extra litres compared with your usual board.
models.rocker_notes: The outline has more curve and the rocker is medium, while the projected diamond tail keeps the board stable and flowing between turns.
models.tail_notes: diamond tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: Hyperlink is an exclusive Pukas x Matt Biolos design for European waves. It is meant to be swift-turning, fast and responsive, evolving from the Link Two concept into a more racey hybrid-performance board. Entry rocker is lifted slightly, tail rocker is lowered, and extra stringer-line curve is added. The double concave is deepened, the deck flattened a touch, and the rails made steeper with a smaller bottom-rail radius. Added forward foil volume and a small beak in the nose help paddling, while the scooped deck in the tail adds rear-foot sensitivity.
models.rocker_notes: Entry rocker is lifted slightly, tail rocker is lowered, and extra stringer-line curve is added. The double concave is deepened, the deck flattened a touch, and the rails made steeper with a smaller bottom-rail radius.
models.tail_notes: Entry rocker is lifted slightly, tail rocker is lowered, and extra stringer-line curve is added. Added forward foil volume and a small beak in the nose help paddling, while the scooped deck in the tail adds rear-foot sensitivity.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Magnetic is a medium-to-long twin-fin design that blends alternative-board flow with new-school performance. It carries a smooth outline with minimal curve, low overall rocker and slightly more tail curve to keep the tail loose and easy to redirect. The bottom mixes concaves with V-bottom for clean hydrodynamics and a smooth efficient ride. It is aimed at surfers of all skill levels who want a longer twin for moderate to larger waves, including more powerful breaks.
models.rocker_notes: It carries a smooth outline with minimal curve, low overall rocker and slightly more tail curve to keep the tail loose and easy to redirect.
models.tail_notes: It carries a smooth outline with minimal curve, low overall rocker and slightly more tail curve to keep the tail loose and easy to redirect.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Mustache is a new-school fish performance concept built around a twin or 2+1 setup. It blends fish speed with high-performance surfboard control through a wider nose outline, smooth taper into the tail, and a distinctive winged mustache swallow tail that sharpens rail engagement and vertical surfing. Recommended for intermediate to advanced surfers in 2-5ft waves, it can be ridden as a 2+1, twin or thruster.
models.rocker_notes: It blends fish speed with high-performance surfboard control through a wider nose outline, smooth taper into the tail, and a distinctive winged mustache swallow tail that sharpens rail engagement and vertical surfing.
models.tail_notes: swallow tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: Performance Mood carries the DNA of a high-performance surfboard and is aimed at surfers chasing vertical lines, rail surfing, speed and modern manoeuvres. It features a clean outline, medium nose and tail width, and a full concave bottom that channels energy toward the tail for quick, responsive direction changes with release and control. It is intended for hardcore surfers who want a modern high-performance board they can tune to their own skill level, preferences and waves.
models.rocker_notes: Performance Mood carries the DNA of a high-performance surfboard and is aimed at surfers chasing vertical lines, rail surfing, speed and modern manoeuvres.
models.tail_notes: It features a clean outline, medium nose and tail width, and a full concave bottom that channels energy toward the tail for quick, responsive direction changes with release and control.

models.manufacturer_evidence: Space Oddity is pitched as a one-board quiver that combines paddle power, versatility, ease and high performance. A forward wide point and healthy nose width carry into a narrower tail, while extra chest thickness boosts paddling. The narrower round pin tail improves control and grip through rail-to-rail surfing. Tail rocker is more pronounced for bigger turns and steeper take-offs, while the medium nose rocker smooths entry. A soft full concave flows into V-bottom and double concave between the fins, and Pukas prefers it as a thruster. It is recommended for surfers of all skill levels across a wide range of conditions.
models.rocker_notes: The narrower round pin tail improves control and grip through rail-to-rail surfing. Tail rocker is more pronounced for bigger turns and steeper take-offs, while the medium nose rocker smooths entry.
models.tail_notes: round pin tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: Water Lion is Chris Christenson's answer for solid European waves like Hossegor and Mundaka. It sits between a performance shortboard and a step-up, adding performance feel to a more powerful-wave board. It starts working in proper hollow head-high surf around 3ft and can be pushed into 6-8ft double-overhead conditions. The outline carries a fuller front end for paddling, while the baby squash tail is tuned for control in solid surf.
models.tail_notes: squash tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: Water Lion Ultra is the sharper, pro-level extension of the Water Lion for solid waves and skilled surfers. It keeps the Water Lion's good-wave DNA but narrows the brief toward the surfers chasing the X factor in serious sessions. Pukas frames it as a board that earned a place in Europe's better surf and then drew interest from pro surfers wanting a more performance-driven version.

The Pyzel Astro Glider is a throwback to the 70's keel fish surfboard, but with a modern take. A shorter, drivier surfboard with plenty of speed, this board works brilliantly in all kinds of waves from 1-4ft and combines smooth turning with quick speed pickup to give you a fun board with plenty of volume underneath you. The Pyzel Astro Glider features: a modern rocker and concave combined with a wider tail to generate electric speed and performance. plenty of volume to get into waves easily. quad fin setup We recommend grabbing one that is about an inch shorted than your regular shortboard and riding it as a quad for a bit of extra spice.
models.rocker_notes: The Pyzel Astro Glider features: a modern rocker and concave combined with a wider tail to generate electric speed and performance.
models.tail_notes: The Pyzel Astro Glider features: a modern rocker and concave combined with a wider tail to generate electric speed and performance.

models.manufacturer_evidence: If you want to go fast you need the Pyzel Astro Pop ElectraLite EPS! The Astro Pop wil give your surfing some extra spark, especially in traditional summer style waves. It's easy to paddle in weaker, slower waves, but it still offers a performance edge. It's extremely fun to ride in good surf with long walls. Built off of the Astro Glider , the Astro Pop has a more pulled in nose and tail. A little wing in the tail helps to pull in the last 4 inches of the tail creating a lot of curve in the outline. This adds maneuverability without sacrificing any speed. The bottom concave is a deep double concave that blends into a vee off the tail. This gets the board on top of the water with ease, even in slower weaker waves. It also makes rail to rail transitions a breeze. The Astro Pop has a mild beak nose which allows the thickness to run all the way into the nose, but it's not overly chunky. We recommend riding the Astro Pop as a quad for ultimate speed, control and drive. It comes stock with a 5 fin setup so you can ride it as a thruster if desired. Ride it short (Jon Pyzel rides his as a 5'5) and about 0.5 to 1 inch wider, and slightly thicker than your everyday shortboard. The new ElectraLite Epoxy Tech boards are built to replicate the ultra-light weight feeling of a Pro board, but with more strength and durability then PU. This a very light board with an excellent strength-to-weig
models.rocker_notes: It also makes rail to rail transitions a breeze.
models.tail_notes: Built off of the Astro Glider , the Astro Pop has a more pulled in nose and tail. A little wing in the tail helps to pull in the last 4 inches of the tail creating a lot of curve in the outline.

models.manufacturer_evidence: If you want to go fast you need the Pyzel Astro Pop XL! The Astro Pop will give your surfing some extra spark, especially in traditional summer style waves. Built off of the Astro, the retro fish weʼve been making for years, the Astro Pop XL has a more pulled in nose and tail, with a little wing to help pull in the last 4 inches of the tail which creates a lot of curve in the outline, adding maneuverability without sacrificing any speed. The bottom has a deep double concave running through to vee off the tail which makes the board get up on top of the water in even slower, weaker waves and still easily tip over onto the rail at high speeds. A mild beak nose helps keep the thickness up into the nose, but itʼs not overly chunky, more like a daily driver type board but with easy paddling aided by the flatter entry rocker. We recommend the Astro Pop XL as a quad, for speed, but it comes stock with a 5-fin setup to maximize versatility.Ride it as short as y

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Crisis Twin is a natural evolution from our Mid Length Crisis, which was typically ridden as a 2+1. We kept seeing people throwing twin keel fins into the forward boxes and realised it made the board a whole different thing, but the fin placement wasn’t optimal for control and performance. So eventually we built the Crisis Twin, a twin fin only version of the MLC with speed and flow as the main objectives. The design was already well suited to being ridden as a twin, a semi- traditional, wide-point forward outline that is sleek and racey for fast, down- the-line drive, but full bodied enough to provide added stability and glide in weaker, slower waves, but we did make a few small tweaks to improve it, pulling in the nose outline a touch to add more curve for more carve, and also adding some nose rocker to free it up a bit in tighter pockets and late drops. Easy paddling, responsive and loose, it has a beautiful foil from beaked nose to round tail, with a rolled deck that leaves the meat in themiddle of the board to help with paddling and nicely thinned out rails for sensitivity and bite. The outline is a long, clean curve, with no hip or wings, so it keeps a smooth flow on rail and through turns. The rocker is a moderate, long, clean curve running from tip to tail without any radical changes or flip. It is designed for fast paddling and to easily generate speed, carry flow,

models.tail_notes: round tail

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Ghost Squash is a version of Pyzel's best seller The Ghost that came about from JJF’s constant desire to experiment with new tweaks on his favourite boards. John’s feedback from his first few sessions on it were simple - the board felt really good, familiar like his favorites, but the squash tail felt fresh, fast, a bit more snappy and still held the rail through all of his turns. He immediately suggested that we should make that version available for everyone to try and we loved the idea, so here it is. We are building the boards directly off of John’s most recent file which has some small thickness adjustments made over the past winter season, but the most significant difference is in the tail. We pushed out the round tail into a semi pulled-in squash which straightens out the last 12 inches in the outline and adds extra lift and creates more of a pivot point than you get out of the classic version. This small change adds a bit more versatility to the Ghost in the lower wave ranges that you might want to ride it in, while still keeping that classic feeling of Ghost magic in good/bigger waves. GHOST SQUASH HIGHLIGHTS: Built directly off of JJF's most recent (2026) Ghost file. Many of the same overall Ghost characteristics that you know and love. Pulled-in Squash tail adds lift in tail area for increased lower end performance without losing hold through turns. Squash tail i
models.rocker_notes: John’s feedback from his first few sessions on it were simple - the board felt really good, familiar like his favorites, but the squash tail felt fresh, fast, a bit more snappy and still held the rail through all of his turns.
models.tail_notes: squash tail
key_trade_offs: Wants cleaner technique and stronger waves
More specialised than an everyday shortboard

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Pyzel Ghost Project 12 is a limited edition version of the Pyzel Ghost that is based on what John John Florence was riding at the start of the 2019 WSL season. It comes with JJF's signature red burger flag logo, his WSL number 12, red pin line rail spray and the Pyzel logo flipped. The Pyzel Ghost Project 12 is only available in JJF's 6'0 and 6'2" dimensions. The Pyzel Ghost was designed to offer performance in hollower, more powerful waves and has proven to work extremely well at reef breaks, dumpy beachies, and point breaks when the swell is 4ft+. The Pyzel Ghost is possibly the first truly high-performance board that works great for surfers of all skill levels and could be considered a one-board quiver for any surf trip with decent to good waves on the menu. Being such a versatile board, it's become one of JJF's favourites for all kinds of waves (Margaret River winner, Bells Allie Oop, J-Bay carves, and even head-high Trestles). It has also become a go-to for many surfers all around the globe. The Pyzel Ghost features: The Ghost outline has the wide point pushed slightly forward of centre, but the nose area is kept down and the tail is slightly pulled in to help the board fit right into the pocket of the wave and hold nicely in the barrel. The thickness is also pushed forward which helps get you into waves easily. The tail and rails are thinned out to help hold through b
models.rocker_notes: It comes with JJF's signature red burger flag logo, his WSL number 12, red pin line rail spray and the Pyzel logo flipped. The tail and rails are thinned out to help hold through barrels and high-speed turns.
models.tail_notes: swallow tail

The Gremlin is a board where performance is key, allowing you to do smooth cutbacks, tight hacks and lofty airs. It's also a great transition board if you are looking to jump off the longboard and onto something a bit shorter. Made using the most advanced construction for maximum durability and performance and featuring genuine Futures fin boxes and durable Eco EPS construction. Funformance design by Jon Pyzel and John John Florence Eco EPS Construction Soft EVA Top Layer Futures Fin boxes Five Fin setup Funformance Technology Each Funformance board that we make is constructed of the highest quality materials and built to perform from progression to performance surfing. Each of our boards features lightweight and durable epoxy/fiberglass construction, a full length recycled EPS foam core with an internal wood stringer, genuine Futures boxes and a soft durable EVA top layer. For maximum performance, each of our boards features hand sanded rails to match the performance profile of each of our board designs. This is a huge advantage of our Funformance boards over traditional softboards as the geometry of our designs is able to be applied to our exclusive technology through detailed hand finishing of each and every board. models.manufacturer_evidence: The official Pyzel product record identifies the Gremlin XL as the support-oriented XL variant of the Gremlin. Quivrr treats it as an all-round hybrid shortboard and applies XL variant guidance separately, so the extra width and foam should be matched to rider size, paddle needs and target volume.

The Happy Twin is a modern take on the classic winged swallow twin fin. We have designed it for down the line speed and lightning fast turns, easy paddling and maximum fun.The Happy Twin features a full outline, with a wide point forward, including a single wing right behind the front fins to create a pivot point for snappy turns and plenty of bite.A flatter rocker ensures easy speed and a tonne of glide in weaker, slower surf. The bottom design uses a double concave within a single, blending into a pronounced Vee off the tail for smoother, more seamless rail to rail transitions.The deck has a special, inset double concave shaped into the chest/front foot area that gives it that already "broken in" comfortable feeling on your very first surf. it also features an old school "beak" nose.A rear fin box (or plug) allows for the use of a stabilizer to add some drive without taking away from the overall feeling of the twins.The Happy Twin is a great addition to your quiver not only smaller surf, but also for a unique fast and free experience in more favorable conditions at nearly all wave types worldwide.We recommend ordering your height or slightly under, around 1" wider and a touch thicker than your shortboard for a slightly increased overall volume and glide.

models.manufacturer_evidence: The Pyzel Highline is the surfboard you want handy for maximizing your performance, even when the waves are less than perfect. Designed to excel in average-to-good waves, and based on the popular Pyzel 74 model (Dane Reynolds's choice for Stab in the Dark). The Pyzel Highline features: The bottom rocker is medium/high, which generates speed and drive, but still allows the board to be loose and responsive. It has a single concave all the way through. This combination of contours and curves generates lift for quick acceleration, holding speed, and drive. The outline wide point is just back from the centre and it has a pronounced hip that adds drive while creating a pivot point that allows for snappy, vertical surfing. The deck is slightly flatter than other Pyzel boards, with more volume pushed out towards the rail. The rail is refined, not boxy, keeping it sensitive and easy to push through turns. To put it simply the Pyzel Highline is a great high-performance surfboard for good to below-average waves. It will help you make the most of your everyday surfs and elevate your surfing performance. We recommend you ride it at anywhere from an inch under to an inch over your height and at your favourite performance surfboard dims and volume.
models.rocker_notes: The Pyzel Highline features: The bottom rocker is medium/high, which generates speed and drive, but still allows the board to be loose and responsive. The deck is slightly flatter than other Pyzel boards, with more volume pushed out towards the rail.
models.tail_notes: The rail is refined, not boxy, keeping it sensitive and easy to push through turns.
